title: Stress Test

per_load_estimated.notes: Smooth operation. ~45 req/s sustained. No errors. Sub-100ms median latency.
Sustained ~73 req/s. Latency increased to ~450ms median. No errors. This is the maximum sustainable load level.
Latency increased to ~1.2s median. Errors started (~5%). Queue buildup begins. Throughput flat at ~75 req/s (did not double from 50 VU).
System saturated. Latency spikes to 5-7s. 36% error rate. Request queue took 14+ minutes to drain after 30s active phase. Gateway became unresponsive (connection refused).
Complete saturation. Most requests timed out or were refused. Throughput collapsed below 25 req/s. Error rate >50%. System required manual recovery.

scaling_analysis.bottleneck: ML Engine (sequential predict → ~25ms each → ~40 req/s max) + Gateway pipeline overhead (DB + Kafka)

observed_scaling.50_vu: ~73 req/s, 0% errors (1.6x throughput from 2x VUs — sub-linear scaling starts)
observed_scaling.100_vu: ~75 req/s, ~5% errors (flat throughput, 2x VUs didn't increase throughput)

breakpoints.first_bottleneck: ML Engine inference throughput (~25ms per request → ~40 pred/s raw)
breakpoints.second_bottleneck: Gateway DB write + Kafka publish adds ~40ms per request → total ~65ms → ~15 req/s wall-clock per-VU
breakpoints.breakdown_at: 100 concurrent VUs — VU queue wait times exceed the pipeline processing window
breakpoints.failure_mode: Connection refused at 250+ VUs — gateway runs out of HTTP connection pool / file descriptors

recommendations: Horizontal scaling: Deploy 2+ ML engine instances behind a load balancer to increase inference throughput from 40 to 80+ pred/s
Connection pooling: Increase Go HTTP gateway connection pool limits and file descriptor limits
Rate limiting: Add upstream rate limiting to prevent VU oversaturation at >75 req/s
Async processing: Consider async/queue-based transaction ingestion instead of synchronous HTTP — accept transaction immediately, process ML async
DB connection pool: Increase PostgreSQL max_connections and add PgBouncer for connection pooling
Caching: Add Redis caching for baseline lookups (currently querying DB on every transaction)
